name: Shopify Admin API
what_it_does: Shopify's Admin API (REST and GraphQL) for managing every aspect of a Shopify store: products, orders, customers, inventory, fulfillment, and more — the foundation for most Shopify app development.
use_cases: Creating and updating products, variants, and inventory from agent workflows
Syncing customer data between Shopify and external CRMs
not_for: Non-Shopify stores (use WooCommerce or BigCommerce APIs instead)
Real-time inventory updates at very high frequency
best_when: An agent needs to manage a Shopify store's operations: orders, products, customers, or fulfillment.
avoid_when: Your store runs on a different platform or you need customer-facing cart/checkout functionality.

auth.notes: Custom apps use access token (API key equivalent). Public apps use OAuth2 with granular scopes. Access token in X-Shopify-Access-Token header. Very agent-friendly for custom apps.

pricing.paid_tiers: Shopify merchant plan required: Basic $39/month, Shopify $105/month, Advanced $399/month
pricing.notes: The API is included with any Shopify merchant plan. Development stores are free for testing. Partner program allows free dev stores.

performance.rate_limits: REST: 40 requests/app/store/second (2 buckets of 40); GraphQL: 1000 cost points/second

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Detailed error arrays with message and field context. Both REST and GraphQL have good error detail. Docs are excellent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: REST API supports idempotency keys via Idempotency-Key header for POST requests. GraphQL mutations do not.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API is being deprecated in favor of GraphQL - new functionality only in GraphQL
Rate limits are per-app-per-store - multi-tenant agents share limits across all stores
GraphQL cost calculation is complex - agents must budget cost points carefully
Webhooks require HMAC-SHA256 verification using the shared secret
Order fulfillment workflow has many status transitions - agents must understand the state machine
